one_time_description: Purchased fishing licenses and permits capitalized as intangible assets at $13,700,000
economic_notes: Nation operates fisheries (DFO operations and fish hatchery/trout farm), Wekoqmaq One Stop retail, gaming revenues from Province of Nova Scotia casino profits, Waycobah Contracting Incorporated (security company), and holds investment in Esukutimkewey Limited Partnership with 12 other First Nations. Exclusive seafood sales agreement with Premium Seafoods Limited. First Nations Finance Authority (FNFA) financing secured by Other Revenues totaling $40,418,473 draw amount.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Three consolidated subsidiaries: Wekoqmaq One Stop, Wekoqmaq Housing Projects, and Wekoqmaq Contracting Incorporated. Major one-time item: $13,700,000 purchased fishing licenses and permits recorded as intangible capital assets. Long-term debt increased significantly from $46.2M to $58.3M, including $12.6M interim FNFA financing due on demand. Net debt position of $34.97M. Proceeds on sale of assets of $3,252,325 is unusual/non-recurring. CEBA loans outstanding. ISC funding figure taken from receivables note as government transfers note content was not fully extracted from document. Total government transfers taken from income statement line. Annual debt service reflects long-term debt repayments only (capital lease repayments of $20,969 additional). No schedule of remuneration included in extracted text.
